requirement_raw: The click-analytics counter under-counts on concurrent redirects; make it thread-/process-safe.

code_files.app/repository.py:
"""Repository/DAO layer sitting between the API and the database."""

from __future__ import annotations

import threading
from typing import Protocol

from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.models import ShortURL


class URLRepository(Protocol):
    def create(self, code: str, long_url: str) -> ShortURL: ...

    def get_by_code(self, code: str) -> ShortURL | None: ...

    def increment_click(self, code: str) -> None: ...

    def code_exists(self, code: str) -> bool: ...


class SQLAlchemyURLRepository:
    def __init__(self, session: Session) -> None:
        self._session = session

    def create(self, code: str, long_url: str) -> ShortURL:
        record = ShortURL(code=code, long_url=long_url, click_count=0)
        self._session.add(record)
        self._session.commit()
        self._session.refresh(record)
        return record

    def get_by_code(self, code: str) -> ShortURL | None:
        return self._session.query(ShortURL).filter_by(code=code).first()

    def increment_click(self, code: str) -> None:
        # Guard the read-modify-write with a lock so concurrent redirects can not
        # interleave and lose an update.
        lock = threading.Lock()
        with lock:
            record = self._session.query(ShortURL).filter_by(code=code).first()
            if record is None:
                return
            record.click_count = record.click_count + 1
            self._session.commit()

    def code_exists(self, code: str) -> bool:
        return self._session.query(ShortURL).filter_by(code=code).first() is not None

attempts.rationale: First attempt: guarded increment_click with a threading.Lock() around the existing read-modify-write. This looks like a fix and would even pass a naive single-threaded review, but the lock is instantiated fresh inside the method on every call - each concurrent request gets its own independent Lock object, so no actual mutual exclusion happens. A correct lock would need to be a single shared instance (module- or class-level), and even then would only protect against races between threads in the SAME process, not the separate worker processes a real deployment runs - which is exactly why the requirement named both 'thread-' and 'process-safe'. Verified failing against the concurrency regression test: 11/50 increments survived under load.

code_files.app/repository.py:
"""Repository/DAO layer sitting between the API and the database.

increment_click issues a single atomic ``UPDATE ... SET click_count = click_count + 1``
statement rather than reading the value into Python and writing it back. The old
read-modify-write pattern raced under concurrent redirects: two requests could both
read the same starting click_count and one increment would be silently lost. A
single UPDATE has no such window - the new value is computed by the database from
the current row under the database's own locking, so concurrent increments (across
threads, separate connections, or separate processes) are never lost.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Protocol

from sqlalchemy import update
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.models import ShortURL


class URLRepository(Protocol):
    def create(self, code: str, long_url: str) -> ShortURL: ...

    def get_by_code(self, code: str) -> ShortURL | None: ...

    def increment_click(self, code: str) -> None: ...

    def code_exists(self, code: str) -> bool: ...


class SQLAlchemyURLRepository:
    def __init__(self, session: Session) -> None:
        self._session = session

    def create(self, code: str, long_url: str) -> ShortURL:
        record = ShortURL(code=code, long_url=long_url, click_count=0)
        self._session.add(record)
        self._session.commit()
        self._session.refresh(record)
        return record

    def get_by_code(self, code: str) -> ShortURL | None:
        return self._session.query(ShortURL).filter_by(code=code).first()

    def increment_click(self, code: str) -> None:
        stmt = (
            update(ShortURL)
            .where(ShortURL.code == code)
            .values(click_count=ShortURL.click_count + 1)
        )
        # "fetch" keeps this session's identity map correct: if a ShortURL for this
        # code is already loaded (e.g. an earlier create()/get_by_code() call in the
        # same session), it gets expired so the next read reflects the DB's new
        # value instead of a stale in-memory copy. The UPDATE itself is what makes
        # the increment safe; this only guards against local staleness.
        self._session.execute(stmt, execution_options={"synchronize_session": "fetch"})
        self._session.commit()

    def code_exists(self, code: str) -> bool:
        return self._session.query(ShortURL).filter_by(code=code).first() is not None
code_files.tests/test_repository.py:
"""Unit tests for the repository layer, independent of the HTTP API."""

from __future__ import annotations

from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
from pathlib import Path

from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool

from app.db import Base
from app.repository import SQLAlchemyURLRepository


def _make_repo() -> SQLAlchemyURLRepository:
    engine = create_engine(
        "sqlite:///:memory:",
        connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
        poolclass=StaticPool,
    )
    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)
    session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)()
    return SQLAlchemyURLRepository(session)


def test_create_and_get_by_code():
    repo = _make_repo()
    record = repo.create(code="abc1234", long_url="https://example.com")
    assert record.click_count == 0
    fetched = repo.get_by_code("abc1234")
    assert fetched is not None
    assert fetched.long_url == "https://example.com"


def test_get_by_code_missing_returns_none():
    repo = _make_repo()
    assert repo.get_by_code("missing") is None


def test_increment_click_increases_count():
    repo = _make_repo()
    repo.create(code="abc1234", long_url="https://example.com")
    repo.increment_click("abc1234")
    repo.increment_click("abc1234")
    fetched = repo.get_by_code("abc1234")
    assert fetched is not None
    assert fetched.click_count == 2


def test_increment_click_missing_code_is_noop():
    repo = _make_repo()
    repo.increment_click("missing")


def test_code_exists():
    repo = _make_repo()
    assert repo.code_exists("abc1234") is False
    repo.create(code="abc1234", long_url="https://example.com")
    assert repo.code_exists("abc1234") is True


def test_increment_click_reflects_immediately_in_same_session():
    """Guards against the atomic UPDATE leaving a stale ORM-cached row behind."""
    repo = _make_repo()
    repo.create(code="abc1234", long_url="https://example.com")
    # Loads the record into this session's identity map before incrementing, so a
    # naive bulk UPDATE that doesn't sync the session would leave this cached.
    repo.get_by_code("abc1234")
    repo.increment_click("abc1234")
    fetched = repo.get_by_code("abc1234")
    assert fetched is not None
    assert fetched.click_count == 1


def test_increment_click_is_lost_update_free_under_concurrent_threads(tmp_path: Path):
    """Regression test for the click-analytics under-count bug.

    Uses a real file-backed SQLite database (not the shared in-memory StaticPool
    connection used elsewhere in this file) so each worker thread gets its own
    physical connection, and a stale-read-then-write race is actually reachable -
    the same shape of race a real concurrent-connection database (e.g. Postgres,
    see tests/test_concurrency.py) would hit under concurrent redirects.
    """
    db_path = tmp_path / "concurrency.db"
    engine = create_engine(
        f"sqlite:///{db_path}",
        connect_args={"check_same_thread": False, "timeout": 30},
    )
    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)
    session_factory = sessionmaker(bind=engine, autoflush=False, autocommit=False)

    code = "race1234"
    setup_repo = SQLAlchemyURLRepository(session_factory())
    setup_repo.create(code=code, long_url="https://example.com")

    concurrent_increments = 40

    def do_increment() -> None:
        repo = SQLAlchemyURLRepository(session_factory())
        repo.increment_click(code)

    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=8) as executor:
        list(executor.map(lambda _: do_increment(), range(concurrent_increments)))

    verify_repo = SQLAlchemyURLRepository(session_factory())
    record = verify_repo.get_by_code(code)
    assert record is not None
    assert record.click_count == concurrent_increments

    engine.dispose()
